texto: So you don't do one night stands. And you're not looking for a boyfriend, or a husband. What do you want, Reina?” I swallowed, my throat suddenly dry. How could one sentence strike with the force of a lightning bolt? “How about you make me an offer?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Tara Leigh que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “So you don't do one night stands. And you're not looking for a boyfriend, or a husband. What do y…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, contemporary-romance, night, com foco em boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: Not only will I discover every single nerve and crevice in your vulnerable, and very naked body, I will devour and exploit every one of your weaknesses. I won’t merely control you, Reina. I will own you.
explicacao: Citação de Tara Leigh sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Not only will I discover every single nerve and crevice in your vulnerable, and very naked body, …” articula reflexao, contemporary-romance, every em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: Not only will I discover every single nerve and crevice in your vulnerable, and very naked body, I will devour and exploit every one of your weaknesses. I won’t merely control you, Reina. I will own you.

texto: I offered my most enticing smile, the one I’d been perfecting for years. Sincere and encouraging, it brimmed with counterfeit confidence. My smile had earned me forgiveness when I deserved none, a seat in first class though I’d only paid for coach.
